content.full_text:
COUNTY COUNSEL

108 COURT STREET • JACKSON, CA 95642-2379 • (209) 223-6366 • FAX (209) 223-4286

July 1, 1991

Ms. Jill Stecher
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, #800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Advice Letter 91-310: Supervisor Davenport

Dear Ms. Stecher:

Today a member of the public called me concerning my June 21, 1991,
letter to Scott Hallabrin regarding the above-referenced
supervisor's request for advice. After reviewing his comment, I
wrote the attached letter which sets forth the person's comment and
my response thereto. Please consider this an addendum to my request
for advice letter (91-310).

Yours very truly,

JOHN F. HAHN
County Counsel

JFH/p

cc: Supervisor Davenport
Catherine Giannini
Ken Berry

COUNTY COUNSEL
108 COURT STREET • JACKSON, CA 95642-2379 • (209) 223-6366 • FAX (209) 223-4286

91.310

July 1, 1991

Mr. Ken Berry
Post Office Box 966
Jackson, CA 95642

Re: Issue of Potential Conflict of Interest: Supervisor Davenport

Dear Ken:

I very much appreciate your telephone call of this morning in which
you pointed out to me what you feel is an error in my opinion in my
June 21 letter to the FPPC's Scott Hallabrin concerning Supervisor
Davenport's outstanding loan from TNH. You pointed out that I had
concluded that Supervisor Davenport did not have a conflict based
on his outstanding debt to TNH because it had not accrued within
the last twelve months. I cited Government Code §7103(c) for that
twelve-month limitation. Said subsection states in pertinent part:

"An official has a financial interest in a decision
within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect...on:...(c) Any source of income...aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in
value provided to, received by or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made."

However, you pointed out that "income" is defined in Government
Code 82030 as including outstanding loans without any limitation on
when the loan accrued. Said section 82030(a) states in pertinent
part:

"(a) 'Income' means...loan. Income also includes an
outstanding loan..."

After studying the matter, I conclude that there is no
inconsistency between Government Code 82030(a) and 87103(c).
"Income" includes outstanding loans as set forth in section
82030(a). However, no conflict arises because subsection 87103(c)
does not state that sources of all income create a conflict, only
certain kinds of income. An outstanding loan not accruing within
twelve months prior to the time a governmental decision is made is
income but not income creating a conflict of interest.

However, due to the importance of the issue involved and your
opinion that my opinion is incorrect, I am forwarding a copy of
this letter to the FPPC as an attachment to my June 21 letter
requesting an opinion.

Again, I thank you for your providing to me your comment regarding
my opinion.

Yours very truly,

[signature]

John F. Hahn
County Counsel

JFH/p

cc:  Supervisor Davenport
     Catherine Giannini
